name: 16x2 LCD Display
tagline: Classic 16-character × 2-row alphanumeric LCD for displaying text and numbers
description: The 16x2 LCD is a widely-used character display that shows 2 rows of 16 characters each. Based on the HD44780 controller, it can be used in 4-bit or 8-bit parallel mode, or with an I2C adapter module (PCF8574) for simple 2-wire control. It's ideal for displaying sensor readings, menus, status messages, and countdowns in Arduino projects.

specs.interface: 4-bit / 8-bit parallel, or I2C via PCF8574 backpack

pinout.name: Contrast
pinout.desc: Contrast adjustment — connect to 10kΩ potentiometer wiper

pinout.name: Read/Write
pinout.desc: Connect to GND for write-only mode

pinout.name: Backlight +
pinout.desc: Backlight LED anode — connect to 5V via 220Ω resistor

pinout.name: Backlight −
pinout.desc: Backlight LED cathode — connect to GND

wiringNote: Use an I2C backpack module (PCF8574) to reduce wiring to just 4 wires: VCC, GND, SDA (A4), SCL (A5). Don't forget the 10kΩ pot on VO for contrast in direct parallel mode.
libraryInstall: For I2C: Arduino IDE → Library Manager → Search 'LiquidCrystal I2C' by Frank de Brabander → Install
commonMistakes: Wrong I2C address (0x27 vs 0x3F) → display stays blank; scan with I2C scanner sketch
Forgetting to call lcd.init() and lcd.backlight() → nothing shows
Not adjusting contrast pot → display shows only blocks or nothing
Connecting VO pin directly to GND → maximum contrast, text barely visible on some displays
